title: chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. suffruticosum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. suffruticosum, also known as Mexican tea or Epazote, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium ambrosioides plant, a member of the amaranth family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Mexican tea include relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Mexican tea can be prepared as a tea by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, or it can be used in herbal preparations such as capsules or tinctures.
